description:
Goal: make `fz2` able to compile the current structured-control surface without reopening old-world lowering or planner machinery.

Signal:
- representative `fz2 run` coverage for `case`, `with`, `receive`, map/struct/bitstring surface, and lambda-capture surface passes on the same fixtures that currently fail
- Compiler2 continues to publish dispatch-owned facts and backend artifacts without calling old `ir_lower` / planner paths
- migration/cutover planning resumes only after this surface gap is closed

Strategy:
- use `dispatch_matrix` as the shared decision engine for source control constructs
- let old-world lowering/runtime code inform durable invariants only; do not import matcher-fn/CPS-era semantic shape
- strengthen Compiler2's structured body/control model first, then lace in the missing data constructors/pattern projections, then lower `case`/`with`/`receive`, then restore outer-surface parity

Why:
- the backend arc is mechanically closed, but `fz2` still rejects real language surface (`Case`, `Receive`, `Map`, `Struct`, `Bitstring`, `Capture`, macro-expanded item/value surface)
- replacement planning is premature until Compiler2 can cover the same control/data surface the fixture matrix treats as ordinary language
title: Compiler2 surface parity: lower structured control through dispatch_matrix
